behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies: correctness depends on what the real model generates from natural-language instructions, not just local parsing logic. A stubbed model can hide signature-selection failures. Rejected Workflow because this behavior checks a single model output, not multi-step agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service with a real LLM on several interface-constrained prompts; assert the produced function signature (names/types/return type) matches the expected contract extracted from the description.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS is module handoff/orchestration logic across generator, test runner, and controller. This can be validated with deterministic stubs; no real OS/network contract is explicitly required, and no model-output quality dependence is required. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about control flow, not correctness of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator + attempt manager + stub generator + stub test runner; configure pass on attempt N and assert attempt order, stop-on-first-pass behavior, and no extra calls after success.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP: this explicitly requires sequential generate/test/refine behavior where later success depends on earlier failure context and multiple model calls. Single-step agentic checks are insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real LLM in the full loop on a known prompt where first attempt commonly fails; verify at least one subsequent attempt (<=3) passes and that progression occurs through real iterative steps.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: This is local module interaction/data handoff (test results -> prompt builder). It does not require real model judgment or real external infra to detect the failure. Rejected Agentic because behavior text only requires prompt content construction, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Connect test-result formatter and prompt-builder modules; feed failing test artifacts and assert the next prompt contains exact failed test names and failure messages.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-step agent behavior: prior failure context must influence a subsequent model decision/output. Deterministic stubs can force code-diff changes without proving real adaptive behavior. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires live attempt content change in response to context.
behaviors.test_description: Run at least two real LLM attempts with failure feedback; assert attempt 2 code materially differs from attempt 1 and reflects referenced failure context, not simple retransmission.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestration/bookkeeping across modules (attempt counter, result aggregation, error shaping). Can be validated with deterministic failing stubs. Rejected System because behavior text does not explicitly require real external contract round-trip or external infra semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with deterministic always-fail stubs; assert exactly 3 attempts, termination after third failure, and structured error payload includes all attempts plus their test-result details.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to non-agentic deterministic stubs and end-to-end local wiring; this is classic integration validation. Rejected System because no explicit requirement to verify real external API/subprocess contract or round-trip external side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline with deterministic stub generator outputs and controlled test outcomes; assert generate->test->bookkeeping transitions and final status are correct across attempts.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Explicit multi-step live loop with real model-dependent improvement over attempts. Success criterion depends on sequential agentic decisions and adaptation, not just one output. Rejected Agentic because single-call agentic testing would miss loop-level recovery behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run full live generate/test/refine/retest flow on a curated hard prompt where attempt 1 fails; assert later attempt succeeds within limit and capture per-attempt progression.
